cases.focus: sitemeta read, unserialize, site-options cache key, missing-option default, and notoptions cache
current-network wrappers, insert/update/delete sitemeta rows, cache updates, and notoptions mutation
batched sitemeta query, cache priming, missing option notoptions, and core network option seed list
pre/default/read filters plus add/update/delete action argument order
non-numeric network IDs fail closed without touching storage
multisite site transient value and timeout stored as network options in sitemeta
expired timeout deletes value and timeout sitemeta rows and returns false
site transient pre-set, expiration, read, short-circuit, set, and delete hooks
external object-cache path uses site-transient group without sitemeta writes

native_boundaries.reason: The probe supplies deterministic sitemeta behavior for network option rows. Full SQL preparation, table-prefix behavior, storage engines, and database errors remain WPHX-305.
site-options and site-transient cache groups use WordPress's native WP_Object_Cache runtime and public wp_cache_* APIs.
The probe enables MULTISITE and loads WP_Network/ms-network.php so get_current_network_id() reaches the real current-network path without a full installed distribution.
site option and site transient hooks remain native PHP callbacks with WordPress-compatible dynamic hook names and argument ordering.
Site transient timeout rows use PHP time(); snapshots normalize absolute timestamps to deterministic markers.

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate side is a copied WordPress oracle source tree until selected multisite option/transient helpers move to Haxe parity candidates.
This fixture constrains wpdb to deterministic sitemeta behavior. Live database multisite storage remains a later integration gate.
This slice covers network options stored in sitemeta via option.php; generic site meta APIs and WP_Site object/query behavior remain separate WPHX-317 work.
The probe loads the needed current-network path but does not claim full installed multisite routing/bootstrap parity.
